top_signals.title: Proposed Iran-U.S. deal would reopen Hormuz strait and lift oil sanctions, Iran state media says
top_signals.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday that the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened as soon as a deal is signed.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: HM Treasury Market Engagement Group
top_signals.summary: A senior‑level forum for financial market participants to share their views on UK-related developments in financial markets, including gilts and sterling, and broader market themes with the Chancellor of the Exchequer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Uber Weighs Delivery Hero Asset Sales to Smooth Full Takeover
top_signals.summary: Uber Technologies Inc. is reaching out to parties interested in Delivery Hero SE’s regional businesses as it works toward a takeover of the German food delivery company that would get regulatory approval, people with knowledge of the matter said.

top_signals.title: Wargaming for Improved Acquisition: What Does It Take?
top_signals.summary:
A few months ago, I attended a panel discussion for a wargame simulating rapid industrial mobilization for armed conflict. Conducted by a leading university, with teams composed of former senior defense officials, the game probed how government and industry collaboration would play out given minimal coordination before the onset of a crisis. On the panel, the defense leaders confessed how infrequently they engaged with industry in real life to plan for a national emergency. This declared lack of public-private planning for large-scale conflict matches what I’ve experienced as a defense planner and wargame developer: Outside of rhetorical claims at annual

International sting shuts down $390M crypto money-laundering ring

alerts.title: Kioxia becomes Japan’s most valuable firm as AI mania goes on
alerts.summary: Shares of the Japanese memory chipmaker surged 7.6% Friday, lifting its market value above ¥44 trillion ($274 billion) just 18 months after its stock market debut.
alerts.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
